title: Citrusy Crisp Venezuelan Soya Bean Stew
description: A hearty, gluten-free Venezuelan stew featuring soya beans, slow-cooked to perfection with a burst of citrus flavors. Perfect for a midnight craving, this dish is both nourishing and delicious.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the soaked and drained soya beans, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Pour in the orange and lime juice, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 45 minutes, or until the soya beans are tender.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro.
Optional: add a squeeze of fresh lime juice and a sprinkle of chopped cilantro to each serving.
Serve with your choice of side dish, such as rice, cornbread, or roasted vegetables.
